pi_items.source: Confirmed via setup_task.sh which cleans up rule 100150 and verifier.py which checks for rule_id 100150 in local_rules.xml.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which writes a synthetic log file to /root/audit_sample.log inside the Wazuh manager container.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which touches /root/replay.log and verifier.py which checks ossec.conf for this location.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which contains target paths /tmp/miner.sh and /var/tmp/.hidden/exploit, and verifier.py which explicitly searches for the regex r"/tmp|/var/tmp".
Confirmed via verifier.py which explicitly checks for level="12" in the rule configuration.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated audit logs injected into the Wazuh container via setup scripts. The target custom rule ID is explicitly verified as 100150 with a severity level of 12. The required regex to match the malicious execution paths is (/tmp|/var/tmp), which corresponds to the seeded log entries for /tmp/miner.sh and /var/tmp/.hidden/exploit. The logs are read from /root/audit_sample.log and must be ingested via /root/replay.log to trigger the alert successfully.
